title: Acampe carinata
taxonomy: Acampe carinata is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This genus belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is part of the order Asparagales. intro: Acampe carinata, also known as the 'Carinate Acampe,' is an orchid species native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing and fever reduction. The bioactive constituents of Acampe carinata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Acampe carinata is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or extract, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its potential health benefits.
